analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet notes COVID-19 caused major disruption to education/training in Europe and invites citizens to participate in an EU public consultation to build inclusive, high-quality digital education (#EUHaveYourSay). Evaluation: EU1 (successful story) – Not directly; it doesn’t showcase past successes or lessons learned beyond noting disruption. EU2 (unity) – Not explicit; no mention of member state solidarity or collective agreement. EU3 (useful for its citizens) – Yes; it promotes an EU public consultation and proactive policy-making to improve digital education for Europeans, showing responsiveness and practical action. EU4 (avant-garde actor) – Partially implied via promoting high-quality digital education, but the tweet doesn’t claim leadership or pioneering status; cautious no. EU5 (global champion of fair causes) – No; not about rights promotion globally. EU6 (contributes to a better world) – No; focus is internal EU education policy, not global contributions. Conclusion: Only EU3 is supported.
